title: CCC Presents: College and Career Center Night for Freshman Families
location: Stevenson High School | Little Theatre & Virtual Meeting via Zoom
description:
The College Career Center will hold its annual night for freshmen families at 7 p.m. on Thursday, April 9. The event will be in person in the Little Theater but can also be  streamed live via Zoom . The slides and recording for this presentation will be available on the College Career Center website within one week of the event for families unable to attend.
 


  
 Stevenson staff will discuss what students can expect during the next three years as they journey toward post-high school opportunities. Students are strongly encouraged to attend with their parent(s) or guardian(s).

title: Day of Dance 2026
description: Please join us for the 2026 Day of Dance:  Mythology: Legends In Motion . All students create and perform their own dances. Different dances will be performed each hour, so feel free to attend multiple times throughout the day. There will be approximately 80 different dances performed throughout the day.

title: Stevenson Band Fundraiser
description:
Support Stevenson High School band students! 

When you dine-in, drive through or carry out from Culver's of Lincolnshire on Friday, April 10, Culver's will donate 25% of group sales to Stevenson's band program.

title: SAGA Dance
description:
We welcome all to a magical evening in our Enchanted Forest!

The SAGA Dance, an all-inclusive event celebrating the LGBTQ+ community and their allies, is on Friday, April 10, from 7 to 9:30 p.m. in the Lower East Glass Commons. Tickets are $11 and can be purchased  here .

title: Evening of Jazz Concert
description: Students from instrumental jazz and a cappella groups will be performing at this annual Evening of Jazz! This concert will feature Low Frequency, Nothing But Treble, Concert Jazz, Polyphonics and Jazz Ensemble.  Click here for the performance program .

title: Military Week
description:
*  Military Presentations:  Representatives from various military branches will be available for student presentations. Sophomores through seniors can sign up for sessions of interest.  Students are asked to complete this interest form as well as a parent/guardian permission slip .

 
 
*  ASVAB Testing:  The Armed Services Vocational Aptitude Battery (ASVAB) will be offered to interested sophomores through seniors. *  Date:  Thursday, April 16
*    Location : Room 5124/5128 (PWC), East Entrance (Parking Lot D)
*  Arrival : 8:30 a.m. with student ID
*  Test Start : 9 a.m.
*   Parent/Guardian Permission Form (LINK) :  Return to the College Career Center by Wednesday, April 15.    
 
*  Questions:  Email  armedservices_liasons@d125.org .

title: The AP and Advanced Art Show
description:
The AP and Advanced Art Show exhibition will take place 7:30 a.m. - 3:30 p.m. on Tuesday April 14 and 7:30 am - 2:30 pm on Wednesday April 15 in Room 2104 and the Upper Forum. The evening reception will take place on Tuesday April 14 from 7-9 p.m.

The show includes work from students enrolled in AP Art, AP Photography & Digital Design, as well as students enrolled in Advanced Photography & Digital Design.

Warning: The artworks in this exhibition were created by AP Art students as part of their sustained investigations. Some pieces explore personal experiences, social issues, and complex themes that may include topics such as identity, mental health, body image, loss or other sensitive subject matter.

Viewer discretion is advised. These works reflect the individual perspectives and artistic inquiries of the students and are intended to encourage thoughtful reflection and dialogue.

title: NHS Spring Blood Drive
description:
Every donation saves 3 lives! National Honor Society is hosting their spring blood drive on Friday, April 17, in the Wrestling Room. Sign up spots are available to donate blood from period 4 through 4:30 pm. Students are encouraged to sign up to donate during their lunch period or after school. They can also sign up during a class period but must also complete a field trip form and receive permission from their teacher to attend.
 * Students must be 16 years of age to donate
* All students participating must have a  signed consent form .
* All students must complete a  field trip form  and receive permission from a teacher to attend. Students are to sign up to attend during their lunch/free period but need permission from the teacher after lunch/free period just in case the donation time runs over. (Example: I sign up to volunteer during my lunch 4th. I need a signature from my 5th hour teacher in case the donation runs into 5th.)

title: Senior Showdown
location: Stevenson High School | Performing Arts Center
description:
Wanna have a good laugh? Are you free at 7 p.m. on Friday April 17? If you answered yes to those questions, then you should come watch 12 seniors perform acts that express their hidden talents. Seniors Showdown is an annual senior talent show organized by Senior Class Board.



Admission is free, just head to the Performing Arts Center for a fun night! Seniors, do not miss this chance of making the best memories with your fellow peers for the last two months of your high school experience.

title: Freshmen Wellness Day
location: Stevenson High School | Patriot Wellness Center
description:
While all the sophomores are taking their Pre-ACTs, all freshmen will spend a fun-filled day with SHS staff to explore the theme of "Live in Community!" Through a variety of activities, freshmen will grow their skills to:
 * Build character and a sense of confidence while appreciating the similarities and differences of others.
* Listen to others and communicate in ways that build empathy and respect.
* Understand what it means to be an effective ally when people are treated with disrespect or injustice.
* Establish and maintain supportive relationships in ways that foster learning.

title: Family Engagement: What to Expect Junior Year
description:
Stevenson Counselor Lisa Scanio will join Family Engagement Coordinator Mara Grujanac for a virtual conversation with families to cover the milestones of junior year. This discussion will cover the key aspects of the junior year experience, including the critical role of communication and collaboration with the Student Services Team, the Post-Secondary Placement Scope meeting and how families can shift to mentoring their teens and empower them to take greater ownership of their decisions and future pathways.

Zoom link:  d125-org.zoom.us/j/99813450764

title: Family Engagement: What to Expect Sophomore Year
description:
Stevenson Counselor Lisa Scanio will join Family Engagement Coordinator Mara Grujanac for a virtual conversation with families that covers the milestones of sophomore year. Topics emphasize the importance of communication and the counselor's role. We will discuss course selection and suggest strategies for having open conversations with your student about their interests and plans. We will also cover the developmental milestones of the sophomore year.

title: NSC Art Festival
description:
The North Suburban Conference Art Festival is an amazing, one-day event that celebrates the artistic achievements of high school students from across the region. Participating schools include Lake Zurich High School, Libertyville High School, Mundelein High School,Adlai E. Stevenson High School, Warren Township High School, Zion-Benton Township High School and Waukegan High School. 

Students selected for this prestigious festival are recognized for their outstanding dedication and talent in the visual arts. The festival provides these young artists an opportunity to showcase their work, compete, and connect with peers from other schools, making it a highlight of the year for the region's artistic community. This year's hosting school is Stevenson High School.

title: Family Engagement: What to Expect Senior Year
description:
Stevenson Counselor Lisa Scanio will join Family Engagement Coordinator Mara Grujanac for a conversation with families to cover the milestones of senior year. We will discuss the milestones of students' senior year at Stevenson including the importance of communication and engagement with the student services team. We will talk about strategies for families to engage in open-ended conversations with their teens.

title: Senior Sunrise
description: Celebrate new beginnings at Senior Sunrise! If you're attending college, feel free to wear your school gear. There will be light breakfast snacks and music. You can also pick up your cap and gown for graduation! Students should bring their ID to be able to pick up their caps and gowns. Ultimately, it will be a great time to hang out with your friends and remember good times from high school at Stevenson!

title: Polish Community Night 2026
location: Stevenson High School | Room 5120/5128 (Patriot Wellness Center)
description:
The   Stevenson Polish Club   organizes Polish Community Night each year to celebrate the Polish heritage of students here at Stevenson, celebrating our Polish Constitution Day, when Poland became the second country in the world after the United States to make a constitution. At this event, everyone from all backgrounds is welcome to come and dance to authentic music, delight in a variety of Polish foods and desserts, and enjoy fun activities like face painting, Polish trivia, games, and more!

Follow   @shspolishclub   on Instagram or read this   multilingual Google Doc     for more information!

title: One Act Plays: Anything Can Happen
description:
The One Acts are a collection of short plays created entirely by Stevenson students who write, direct, design, produce, crew and perform each of the six stories. The 2026 One Acts theme is "Anything Can Happen" and culminates the year's play development partnership between Scripts Studio and Patriot Theatre Company. Don't miss this entertaining, exciting and student-centered event!

Warnings: Mature language and themes, depiction of weapons and flashing lights.

title: Spring Choir Concert
description: Join us for our final choir concert of the year, featuring all three of our choirs, all three of our a cappella groups, and most importantly, featuring the Senior Class of 2026. A short awards ceremony will begin around 6:30 p.m. with the concert starting at 7 p.m. Come hear lots of great music, ranging from ABBA to Destiny's Child, South Africa to Appalachia and more!

title: Family Engagement: How to Start: Discovering Your Life's Work
description:
Work is how we spend much of our time. It's our engine of progress: how cancer therapies are invented, political campaigns won, thrilling art created and matched with an audience. Instead of letting cynicism take over, Kantor identifies two principles to help young people discover their life's work: craft and need. By pairing the two, they can navigate tough, sensitive choices: how to think about money. How much risk to take on. When to buck what others are saying.
Powerful and provocative, How to Start is a statement of faith for young people as they make their way through uncertain times, offering wisdom, strategy, and a set of aspirations to launch their careers and last their whole lives.
To register:  www.bit.ly/KantorFANSponsor

title: Mélange and Repertory General Auditions
description: General auditions for Mélange and Repertory Dance Companies will be held May 13-14 from 3:45-6 p.m. in Room 2112. Be sure to sign up in advance. Come prepared by learning the audition phrase (released May 8) and creating a strong 45-60 second original solo in jazz or contemporary. Results will be posted the week of May 18th, and all selected dancers are expected to attend a mandatory meeting on May 19. Company placement will take place in August, with dancers cast into either Mélange or Repertory at that time.
